params.text:
The "rights" of medication administration

The "rights" are a simple safety checklist used every time a medicine is given. Running through them helps prevent the most common errors. If any right cannot be confirmed, the medicine should not be given until it is checked.

params.title: Tap a card to flip it
params.description: The rights of medication administration. Read the right, then flip to see what it means.

dialogs.answer: Confirm the medicine belongs to the individual in front of you, using their name and details on the MAR chart. Never assume.
Check the name and strength against the MAR chart, and give the exact amount prescribed — no more, no less.
Give at the prescribed time and by the correct route (for example by mouth, on the skin, or inhaled). The route affects how the medicine works.
A person with capacity can decline any medicine. Never force it. Record the refusal and report it, as a missed dose may need follow-up.
Record the medicine on the MAR chart straight after it is taken. If it is not recorded, it is treated as not given.
